title_of_original: State Aid SA.58880 (2020/FC) – Sweden – Aid to recapitalise Swedavia
case_description: [1] Aarhus Midtjylland Airport A/S (“AMA”), a privately owned operator of Aarhus Midtjylland Airport in eastern Jutland, [2] lodged a formal complaint under EU State aid rules. [3] The complaint concerned an alleged State aid measure granted by the Danish State to Dansk Luftfart A/S ("DL A/S"), [4] a fully state-owned company operating a network of five airports across Denmark. [5] The act in question was a payment of DKK 2.6 billion (approximately EUR 250 million), [6] carried out on 5 November 2020, following a decision by the Danish Government on 26 October 2020. [7] In its decision, the government stated that it wanted to add capital to the company to overcome a period of liquidity shortfall that Dansk Luftfart was suffering from [8] due to COVID-19-related travel restrictions since 14 March 2020. [9] These restrictions were significantly affecting air traffic, [10] including routes operated or planned to be operated at Dansk Luftfart’s airports. [11] The payment was made by the Danish Agency for Public Finance and Management (Statens Administration) pursuant to Danish Law 2020:145 [12] and was financed from the general budget of the Danish State. [13] Given the uncertainty of the COVID-19 situation, and under the assumption that restrictions would be even stricter in the upcoming 2020 winter, [14] experts doubted that the payment would help resolve the liquidity issues and return the company to profitability. [15] They assumed that a possible return of the payment, if even possible, could take at least ten years and [16] that the risk and return horizon are far below the threshold a private investor would accept. [17] On 8 November 2022, Denmark informed the European Commission that it had recovered part of the payment from Dansk Luftfart. [18] Specifically, it had recovered DKK 935.2 million, [19] which included DKK 915.8 million of the principal and DKK 19.4 million in interest. [20] On 20 November 2024, Denmark notified the Commission of an additional recovery of DKK 211.5 million (plus interest).
